_document_type: physrisk MCP result cache — staged for demo so `assess_physical_risk` / `calculate_climate_var` lookups resolve instantly
_note: In a live RSRA run, physrisk stays a LIVE call (see README). This file mirrors the exact shape those tools return so the demo can cite it without a network round-trip.

hazards.finding: Denver metro sits in 'Hail Alley' — front-range severe-hail frequency is projected to increase, shortening roof and HVAC condenser replacement cycles.
South Platte basin allocation tightening; landscaping and common-area water costs trending up.
9 additional days above 95F by 2050 (NOAA); modest cooling-load increase for electric AC.
FEMA Zone X — outside the 500-year floodplain; minimal exposure.
Urban infill location, more than 8 miles from the nearest WUI boundary.

calculate_climate_var.primary_driver: Severe convective storm / hail — roof & building envelope
calculate_climate_var.methodology: physrisk NGFS/IPCC hazard overlay applied to asset value; EAL discounted over hold period

operational_risk.note: Operational indices are separate from the financial Climate VaR above — they describe likelihood of service disruption (cooling capacity strain, irrigation curtailment), not dollar loss.

insurance_financing_implications: Colorado front-range hail losses have driven several carriers to raise wind/hail deductibles (percentage-of-value rather than flat dollar) or non-renew garden-style multifamily with original 2006-era roofing.
No FEMA high-risk flood zone designation — Fannie Mae / Freddie Mac flood-exposure exclusions do not apply.
Flag current P&C policy's wind/hail deductible structure for broker review during due diligence.
